frameworks.name: decision stopping rules
frameworks.one_line: Criteria that determine when to commit to a perceptual choice.

frameworks.name: sequential evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Temporal weighting of sensory information over the trial.

frameworks.name: post-error adjustment
frameworks.one_line: Strategy updates after errors modulate subsequent stopping behavior.

core_question: How do subjects adjust decision stopping rules to balance premature versus late commitments in an auditory change detection task?
key_assumptions: Decision commitment is governed by a stopping rule that can be flexibly controlled
Stopping rules trade off premature against late decisions
Temporal weighting of sensory evidence is estimable from behavior
manipulations: Task conditions that invoke different decision stopping rules
key_findings: Different stopping rules did not produce systematic differences in temporal weighting of sensory evidence
Bidirectional post-error adjustments depend on the type of error committed
Post-error adjustments reduce the probability of consecutive same-type mistakes
interpretations: Stopping-rule control and sensory-evidence weighting are dissociable components of decision making
Error-type-specific strategy updates serve as an adaptive control mechanism over decision commitment
